verses.text: God shows Amos locusts devouring the grass; he pleads, 'O Lord GOD, forgive,' and the LORD relents. Intercession works. The prayer of one man stays the hand of judgment.
God shows Amos locusts devouring the grass; he pleads, 'O Lord GOD, forgive,' and the LORD relents. Intercession works. The prayer of one man stays the hand of judgment.
God shows Amos locusts devouring the grass; he pleads, 'O Lord GOD, forgive,' and the LORD relents. Intercession works. The prayer of one man stays the hand of judgment.
God shows a devouring fire; again Amos cries, 'O Lord GOD, cease,' and the LORD relents. Mercy at prayer. God hears the plea of His servant for the people.
God shows a devouring fire; again Amos cries, 'O Lord GOD, cease,' and the LORD relents. Mercy at prayer. God hears the plea of His servant for the people.
God shows a devouring fire; again Amos cries, 'O Lord GOD, cease,' and the LORD relents. Mercy at prayer. God hears the plea of His servant for the people.
God stands with a plumbline, declaring He will measure Israel and spare no more; the high places shall be desolate. The standard set. God tests His people by His own straight rule.
God stands with a plumbline, declaring He will measure Israel and spare no more; the high places shall be desolate. The standard set. God tests His people by His own straight rule.
God stands with a plumbline, declaring He will measure Israel and spare no more; the high places shall be desolate. The standard set. God tests His people by His own straight rule.
Amaziah, priest of Bethel, accuses Amos to the king: 'the land is not able to bear all his words.' Truth resented. Faithful preaching is branded as conspiracy by the guilty.
Amaziah, priest of Bethel, accuses Amos to the king: 'the land is not able to bear all his words.' Truth resented. Faithful preaching is branded as conspiracy by the guilty.
Amaziah tells Amos to flee to Judah and not prophesy at Bethel, 'for it is the king's chapel.' Religion of the court. He prefers a religion that serves the king to one that serves God.
Amaziah tells Amos to flee to Judah and not prophesy at Bethel, 'for it is the king's chapel.' Religion of the court. He prefers a religion that serves the king to one that serves God.
Amos answers he was 'no prophet,' but a herdman, till 'the LORD took me... and said... Go, prophesy.' A called man. God's commission, not human training, makes the true prophet.
Amos answers he was 'no prophet,' but a herdman, till 'the LORD took me... and said... Go, prophesy.' A called man. God's commission, not human training, makes the true prophet.
Because Amaziah forbade God's word, judgment falls on his house, and Israel shall go captive. Silencing punished. To oppose God's message is to invite His sentence.
